title: Docker Compose app has invalid /etc/hosts
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Docker Compose machines do not have each others' hosts specified in `/etc/hosts` file, breaking connectivity between services.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Clone https://github.com/abbradar/hello-docker-compose (this is example app from https://docs.docker.com/compose/gettingstarted/ , put into a repository for convenience);

2. Run `docker-compose -H unix:/run/podman/podman.sock up`

3. Try `http://localhost:5000`

**Describe the results you received:**

Internal error:
```
web_1    | socket.gaierror: [Errno -2] Name does not resolve
```

Analyzing `/etc/hosts` on `web` machine shows that it has no entries for `redis` host:

```
127.0.0.1 localhost
::1 localhost
127.0.0.2 abbradarbook
::1 abbradarbook
10.89.0.12	8bb1ff2a7281 test_web_1
10.89.0.1 host.containers.internal
```

If I lookup IP of `redis` host and then ping it from `web`, it works:

```
$ sudo podman exec test_redis_1 ip addr
iip/data # ip addr
...
    inet 10.89.0.11/24 brd 10.89.0.255 scope global eth0
$ sudo podman exec test_web_1 ping 10.89.0.11
PING 10.89.0.11 (10.89.0.11): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.89.0.11: seq=0 ttl=64 time=0.275 ms
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

`redis` host is available in `/etc/hosts` on `web` machine, website working properly.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

May be related to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12003, but this bug also happened on 3.3.1.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Tue Jan  1 03:00:00 1980
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL

  slirp4netns:
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

(nothing similar on NixOS)

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

NixOS 21.11 (unstable) machine.

comments.body: The container names are not put in /etc/hosts as this is not scalable. Instead a dns server is used. Do you have the dnsname cni plugin installed?
@Luap99 No, I didn't - it works now. I'm not sure I've seen mentions of that before - is this documented somewhere?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think we can close this, given it seems to be a configuration issue
